extraction.one_line_summary: POGAČAR attacked with 500m remaining on Los Machucos summit finish, took second stage win and vaulted into top-3 GC
tactical_patterns: POGAČAR positioned at wheel of ROGLIČ on final 1.5km summit, exploited lead-out rather than making distance attack
LÓPEZ Miguel Ángel cracked on Los Machucos steep gradients, revealing weakness on explosive finishes versus steady tempo climbs
QUINTANA and VALVERDE stayed close on final climb despite massive early breakaway, prioritizing GC defense over stage hunting
Twenty-nine rider breakaway composition shows stage profile too selective; pure sprinters absent, teams unable to control monster breaks

rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: at 20 years old, second stage win plus demonstrated tactical maturity by drafting ROGLIČ before sprinting
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: neutral: controlled race in red jersey but losing time bonuses to POGAČAR signals tightening GC battle ahead
rider_signals.LÓPEZ Miguel Ángel: negative: lost over a minute on Los Machucos, dropped to fifth overall, confirms inability to respond to explosive accelerations
rider_signals.POELS Wout: positive: dominated pacemaking on penultimate climbs for 20+ kilometers, emerged as critical ROGLIČ domestique on mountain stages
rider_signals.KELDERMAN Wilco: negative: dropped on Los Machucos final climb, losing 1:08 exposes limitations on steepest gradients despite earlier consistency
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: neutral: maintained contact with leaders but unable to contest sprint, showing defensive GC riding rather than stage ambition
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: positive: attacked earlier on Los Machucos, finished alongside VALVERDE at 27 seconds, demonstrating improved form mid-race
rider_signals.LATOUR Pierre: positive: third place finish from early breakaway shows ability to survive selection and sprint after elite climbers attack
rider_signals.GILBERT Philippe: neutral: present in 29-rider breakaway but commentary shows team calculation prioritizing king of mountains over stage win
